req.title: System-Admin deletes org-announcement
req.statement: An authenticated system-admin shall be able to delete a org-announcement through the REST API. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers DELETE on /api/org-announcements by a system-admin. Key test dimensions are: (1) happy-path deletion with audit verification (EP-positive), (2) unauthenticated request returning 401 (NEG), (3) forbidden role returning 403 (DT), (4) missing mandatory field returning 400 (NEG), (5) deleting an already-deleted announcement to cover state transition (STC), and (6) boundary on announcement ID at minimum valid value (BVA). Together these cover all stated error pathways and the positive outcome including audit record assertion.

tests.name: test_admin_delete_announcement_success
preconditions: A valid org-announcement exists with known id
actions: Obtain admin auth token via <get_admin_token> fixture
Create a test org-announcement via <create_announcement> helper to get announcement_id
Send DELETE /api/org-announcements/{announcement_id} with valid admin token
Query audit log via <get_audit_record> helper for the deleted resource
expected: Response body contains the deleted entity id
Audit record exists referencing announcement_id and action DELETE
trace: R-WEB-051: response 200 or 201 with entity id
R-WEB-051: audit record written on success
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_admin_delete_announcement_success(admin_token, base_url, create_announcement, get_audit_record):
    announcement_id = create_announcement(admin_token)
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_token}"}

    resp = requests.delete(
        f"{base_url}/api/org-announcements/{announcement_id}",
        headers=headers,
    )

    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Unexpected status: {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert str(announcement_id) in str(body), "Entity id missing from response"

    audit = get_audit_record(resource_id=announcement_id, action="DELETE")
    assert audit is not None, "Audit record not written"
    assert audit["action"] == "DELETE"
    assert str(audit["resource_id"]) == str(announcement_id)

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_delete_returns_401
preconditions: A valid org-announcement exists with known id
expected: Announcement is NOT deleted (resource still exists)
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_unauthenticated_delete_returns_401(base_url, admin_token, create_announcement, resource_exists):
    announcement_id = create_announcement(admin_token)

    resp = requests.delete(
        f"{base_url}/api/org-announcements/{announcement_id}"
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 401, f"Expected 401, got {resp.status_code}"
    assert resource_exists(admin_token, announcement_id), "Resource was deleted by unauthenticated request"

tests.name: test_forbidden_role_delete_returns_403
preconditions: User is authenticated with role non-admin (e.g. viewer)
A valid org-announcement exists with known id
actions: Obtain non-admin auth token via <get_viewer_token> fixture
expected: Announcement is NOT deleted (resource still exists)
trace: R-WEB-051: error pathway - 403 if role forbidden
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_forbidden_role_delete_returns_403(base_url, admin_token, viewer_token, create_announcement, resource_exists):
    announcement_id = create_announcement(admin_token)
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {viewer_token}"}

    resp = requests.delete(
        f"{base_url}/api/org-announcements/{announcement_id}",
        headers=headers,
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 403, f"Expected 403, got {resp.status_code}"
    assert resource_exists(admin_token, announcement_id), "Resource was deleted by forbidden role"

tests.name: test_delete_missing_mandatory_field_returns_400
actions: Send DELETE /api/org-announcements/ with no announcement id in path (omit mandatory id)
expected: Response body indicates validation or not-found error
trace: R-WEB-051: error pathway - 400 on validation error
R-WEB-051: mandatory fields must be validated

tests.name: test_delete_already_deleted_announcement_state_transition
preconditions: A valid org-announcement exists and has already been deleted (terminal state)
actions: Create and immediately delete an org-announcement to put it in deleted state
Send DELETE /api/org-announcements/{announcement_id} a second time with the same admin token
expected: Second DELETE response status code is 404 or 409
No duplicate audit record written for the second attempt
trace: R-WEB-051: state transition - resource in deleted state cannot be deleted again
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_delete_already_deleted_announcement_state_transition(base_url, admin_token, create_announcement, get_audit_records_count):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_token}"}
    announcement_id = create_announcement(admin_token)

    first = requests.delete(
        f"{base_url}/api/org-announcements/{announcement_id}",
        headers=headers,
    )
    assert first.status_code in (200, 201), f"First delete failed: {first.status_code}"
    count_after_first = get_audit_records_count(resource_id=announcement_id, action="DELETE")

    second = requests.delete(
        f"{base_url}/api/org-announcements/{announcement_id}",
        headers=headers,
    )
    assert second.status_code in (404, 409), f"Expected 404/409, got {second.status_code}"
    count_after_second = get_audit_records_count(resource_id=announcement_id, action="DELETE")
    assert count_after_second == count_after_first, "Duplicate audit record written on second delete"

tests.name: test_delete_announcement_boundary_minimum_valid_id
preconditions: An org-announcement exists whose id equals the minimum valid positive integer (1)
actions: Ensure announcement with id=1 exists via <ensure_announcement_exists> helper
Send DELETE /api/org-announcements/1 with valid admin token
expected: Audit record written for resource_id=1 and action DELETE
trace: R-WEB-051: BVA on announcement id lower boundary (id=1)
R-WEB-051: positive outcome - deletion succeeds at boundary value
R-WEB-051: audit record written on success
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_delete_announcement_boundary_minimum_valid_id(base_url, admin_token, ensure_announcement_exists, get_audit_record):
    boundary_id = 1
    ensure_announcement_exists(admin_token, boundary_id)
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_token}"}

    resp = requests.delete(
        f"{base_url}/api/org-announcements/{boundary_id}",
        headers=headers,
    )

    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Unexpected status: {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert str(boundary_id) in str(body), "Boundary entity id missing from response"

    audit = get_audit_record(resource_id=boundary_id, action="DELETE")
    assert audit is not None, "Audit record not written for boundary id"
